ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not cover branded biscuit products. Closest parent would be 'sandwich biscuit' or 'chocolate-coated biscuit' — no exact URI available for Arnott's Tim Tam Original.
ontology.foodex2_description: Biscuits and crackers (closest FoodEx2 parent category for chocolate-coated sweet biscuits)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for branded chocolate-coated malted biscuit sandwiches. A000E (Biscuits and crackers) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded biscuit products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 biscuits (approximately 35 g), providing approximately 170 kcal, 15 g sugar, and 8.5 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Arnott's Tim Tam Original — Nutrition Information Panel (Singapore-sold product label)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nutrition Information Panel on the Singapore-sold Arnott's Tim Tam Original product label. Values are per 100 g and per 35 g serving as declared on pack. This constitutes a USDA Branded Food-equivalent primary source for a commercial packaged product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of chocolate-coated malted biscuit sandwich — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~62 (medium) sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for chocolate-coated malted biscuit sandwiches, which is the closest analogous product category to Arnott's Tim Tam Original. GL per 35 g serving calculated as (62 × 22.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 13.6. Available carbohydrate per serving = 62.9 g/100 g × 35 g / 100 = 22.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content is moderate at 229 mg/100 g (80 mg per 35 g serving); primary cardiovascular concerns are saturated fat and sugar rather than sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Tim Tam Original is low in sodium per serving (80 mg per 2 biscuits) and does not pose a significant sodium burden for hypertensive patients when consumed as a single stated serving, though the high saturated fat content (5.6 g per serving) warrants attention for overall cardiovascular risk management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar content (42.9 g/100 g; 15 g per 35 g serving) and medium GI (62) with GL ~14 per serving; intense palatability makes adherence to the stated 2-biscuit serving clinically difficult.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Tim Tam as an occasional indulgence only — the 15 g sugar and GL of ~14 per stated 2-biscuit serving are significant, and the high palatability of the chocolate-malt-cream combination makes overconsumption (4–6 biscuits in one sitting) a documented clinical risk in Singapore dietitian counselling.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high saturated fat (16 g/100 g; 5.6 g per 35 g serving) from milk chocolate coating and palm oil cream filling, combined with high sugar (42.9 g/100 g).
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tim Tam Original has the highest saturated fat content among common packaged biscuits (16 g/100 g), primarily from palm oil and milk chocolate; patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to the stated 2-biscuit serving on an infrequent basis and account for the saturated fat contribution within their daily limit of less than 7% of total energy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Arnott's Tim Tam Original is Australia's best-selling chocolate biscuit, first launched in 1964 and named after a racehorse. It consists of two malted chocolate biscuit layers sandwiching a chocolate cream filling, entirely enrobed in milk chocolate. The texture is crisp-then-yielding: the outer chocolate shell snaps, the biscuit layers are lightly crunchy with a malt flavour, and the cream centre is smooth and sweet. In Singapore, Tim Tam is the premium imported biscuit of choice, available at Cold Storage, FairPrice Finest, and convenience stores, and is consistently ranked among the most popular food products brought back from Australia by Singaporean travellers. Clinically, Tim Tam has one of the highest saturated fat contents among packaged biscuits (16 g/100 g from chocolate coating and palm oil cream), very high sugar (42.9 g/100 g = 15 g per 2-biscuit serving), and a medium glycemic index of approximately 62 with a glycemic load of ~14 per serving. The intense palatability of the chocolate-malt-cream combination makes the stated 2-biscuit serving extremely difficult to respect in practice; Singapore dietitians specifically counsel diabetic patients about the risk of consuming 4–6 biscuits in a single sitting. Each 35 g serving provides approximately 170 kcal, 8.5 g fat, 5.6 g saturated fat, 22 g carbohydrate, and 15 g sugar. Arnott's is owned by Campbell Soup Company.
common_user_queries: how many calories in 2 Tim Tams

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Arnott's Tim Tam Original Singapore-sold product Nutrition Information Panel (exact product label match). GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for the closest analogous product category (chocolate-coated malted biscuit sandwich). All core macros and secondary nutrients are fully populated from the verified product label.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown is not declared on the Singapore product NIP and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact branded product as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content is not declared on the product label and is not available in approved sources for this exact product. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per product label declaration. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed 0.0 per product label declaration. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate-coated malted biscuit sandwiches — not a direct measurement of the Arnott's Tim Tam Original SKU. GL per serving calculated as (62 × 22.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 13.6, where available carbohydrate per 35 g serving = 22.0 g (62.9 g/100 g × 0.35). This is a packaged commercial product (ultra-processed); the record type is generic/branded. Halal certification applies to the Singapore-sold product. Hindi local name added as 'टिम टैम बिस्कुट' based on common transliteration and usage for biscuits in Hindi-speaking contexts, ensuring compliance with multilingual requirements for Singaporean dishes. Q8 fix (iteration 3): all strings present in aliases_by_language — including romanized entries 'Tí Mǔ Tǎ Mǔ Qiǎo Kè Lì Bǐng Gān' and 'Ṭim Ṭēm Piskaṭ' — have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
